A standout shoyu ramen spot in Tokyo. Their signature menu is the “Special Shoyu Ramen.” Built on a chicken-based broth blended with multiple soy sauces, the first sip brings out the richness of chicken oil, deep umami from the soy sauce, and a subtle sweetness. As you continue, the flavors finish clean and balanced. The charcoal-grilled chashu adds a gentle smoky aroma that naturally melts into the broth✨ The straight noodles hold the soup well, delivering its flavor all the way through each bite. The chashu comes in a variety of cuts, each prepared differently—from rich, fatty pieces to lighter, more delicate textures—letting you enjoy a contrast of juiciness and mouthfeel. “Dog lovers” vs dog lovers when it comes to greeting dogs! ETA: “dog lovers” could also be replaced with “uniformed, uneducated, and/or inconsiderate dog lovers” vs “informed, educated, and considerate dog lovers” if that makes you more comfortable 🫶 One of my biggest pet peeves is people baby talking to my dog and pretending like I’m interrupting? Bizarre! And in case you didn’t know because it’s something that’s been so normalized, putting your hands in dogs’ faces is unsafe. Dogs don’t need to sniff you up close like that and it’s putting unneeded pressure on them (often leading to bites). The best way to greet a dog is calm, neutral, and honestly just ignoring them. If you want to say hi to a dog, ask the owner first. Try to make yourself small by turning to the side and crouching. Allow the dog to approach you first. Some dogs are super friendly and love people! This doesn’t mean it’s ok to pet or approach without asking their handler! The safest way to greet any dog is by admiring from afar. Admittedly, if I know someone’s dog well, I might be a bit of the former “dog lover” and be excitedly greeting them 😂 It’s different when you know the dog! This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss

Creamy Cheesy Buldak Ramen 🍜🧀🔥 Ingredients 1 pack Buldak ramen 1 slice baby Swiss cheese 1 slice American cheese 1 slice Monterey Jack cheese 2 tbsp mayo Buldak sauce packet Buldak powder packet Chili oil 2 cloves garlic, minced 2 tbsp chopped green onions ½ cup heavy cream or milk Cooked ramen noodles Toppings Fried egg Mini sausages Dumplings Extra green onions Extra chili oil Instructions Cook the Buldak noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside. In a pan over low heat, add the baby Swiss, American, and Monterey Jack cheese slices. Add the mayo, Buldak sauce, Buldak powder, chili oil, minced garlic, and green onions on top of the cheese. Mix everything together until the cheese melts into a thick spicy paste. Pour in the heavy cream or milk and stir until smooth and creamy. Add the cooked noodles and toss until fully coated in the sauce. Top with fried egg, dumplings, mini sausages, extra green onions, and more chili oil if desired. Enjoy hot and creamy  #buldak #buldakramen #eat #food  #Foodie

Ultra Creamy Buldak Ramen 🍝🥟 Ingredients 1 pack Buldak ramen 1–2 tbsp butter 2 cloves garlic (minced) 1 tbsp Kewpie mayo Buldak sauce packet Buldak powder packet 1 tbsp chili oil ¾–1 cup heavy cream Mozzarella cheese Dumplings Sausages Green onions Sesame seeds Extra chili oil (for topping) Instructions Cook noodles until al dente, then drain and set aside. In a pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant. Add Kewpie mayo, Buldak sauce, and powder packet. Mix well until it turns thick and glossy. Add chili oil and stir. Pour in heavy cream and mix until smooth, creamy, and slightly thickened (that rich orange color 🔥). Add noodles and toss until fully coated. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese on top and let it melt into the sauce. Add dumplings and sausages on top. Cover and let everything heat through. Finish with green onions, sesame seeds, and extra chili oil drizzle. . #buldak #buldakramen #eat #cook #Foodie
